title: Historical Ceremonial Guard at Casa Rosada Museum in the City of Buenos Aires Argentina
description: City of Buenos Aires, Buenos Aires: Audrey Bergner and Daniel Bergner stand alongside a ceremonial soldier of the Granaderos a Caballo, the elite regiment founded by José de San Martín, inside the halls of the Casa Rosada Museum. The composition captures a moment of institutional reverence as the visitors pose beside the uniformed guard, whose ornate headgear and period-accurate regalia starkly contrast with the sleek, modern museum architecture. Soft, diffused light filters through the overhead glass panels, casting an even glow that illuminates the vibrant blue of the museum displays and the rugged, historic brickwork integrated into the interior design. Daniel gestures with an approving thumb, bridging the gap between contemporary tourism and the weight of national pride represented by the silent, steadfast soldier. The juxtaposition of a modern family visit against the backdrop of colonial-era heritage highlights the living history that permeates the capital city. Every detail, from the crisp lines of the soldier's posture to the reflective surfaces of the glass vitrines, is rendered with clarity, emphasizing the meticulous curation of the site. Such encounters allow travelers to engage directly with the foundational symbols of Argentine independence and the ongoing dedication to preserving national memory. By integrating personal exploration with civic milestones, the image underscores the profound intersection of individual experience and collective history within South American urban landscapes. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
